Name Popularity Comparison

Compare any two first names side-by-side. See which one is more popular, how each has trended over decades, and which feels more unique today.

Want to compare 3 or 4 names on one chart? Switch to the Trend Visualizer. Just want to look up one name? Try the Popularity Checker.

Try a preset comparison

vs

What is a Name Comparison Tool?

A name comparison tool takes two first names and renders their popularity statistics side-by-side. The HowManyOfMe comparison shows three core attributes for each name — global rank, estimated bearer count, and decade-by-decade trend — plus a "More Popular Name" callout that quantifies the difference. Based on US Social Security Administration data and global registries, it's the fastest way to settle baby-name debates with data instead of opinion. Want to step back and find out how many people share your name?

Key Features and Attributes

Side-by-side bearer cards

Two large stat cards showing rank and global count, designed for instant scanning.

Dual decade bars

Each decade row shows both names back-to-back, so trend divergence is obvious.

Winner callout

Auto-calculates which name has more bearers and the exact difference.

Deep-link sharing

Comparison URL preserves both names so partners can open the same view.

Cross-tool links

One click jumps to the full statistics page for either name.

Identical-scale charts

Both bars use the same 0–100 normalized scale — no misleading visuals.

Who Uses This Tool?

Couples deciding between two finalists

Scenario: Stuck choosing between 'Emma' and 'Olivia'.

Result: See Emma has ~1.9M bearers vs Olivia's ~1.4M — both top-tier, Emma slightly more saturated.

Authors picking sibling names

Scenario: Want two character names that feel from the same era.

Result: Compare decade peaks to confirm both names dominated the same decade (e.g. 1980s).

Linguists studying name diffusion

Scenario: Researching how a celebrity baby name spreads vs a control name.

Result: Compare a treatment name (e.g. 'North') against a similar non-celebrity name to isolate the effect.

Family debates

Scenario: Grandparents say 'Margaret' is timeless, parents say 'Mia' is the modern equivalent.

Result: Comparison shows Margaret's century-long stability vs Mia's recent surge — both right.

Worked Examples

Input: "Liam vs Noah"

Finding: Liam ranks #1 since 2017, Noah held the spot 2013–2016.

Insight: Both ultra-popular; Liam now has slight edge but they're effectively tied.

Input: "Charlotte vs Amelia"

Finding: Both peaked in 2020s, Amelia ~10% more bearers globally.

Insight: Either name guarantees your child meets a same-named peer in school.

Input: "Atticus vs Theodore"

Finding: Theodore ranks ~#10, Atticus ~#270.

Insight: Atticus reads as distinctive while still familiar; Theodore is mainstream.

✓ Pros

  • ✓ Settles 'which name is more popular' arguments in one click
  • ✓ Visual decade bars make trend differences obvious
  • ✓ Free, unlimited, no signup
  • ✓ Deep-linkable URLs for sharing with a partner
  • ✓ Both names rendered on identical scales

✗ Cons

  • ✗ Limited to two names — use Trend Visualizer for 3 or 4
  • ✗ No phonetic or meaning-based comparison
  • ✗ Rare names (<100 bearers) may show as essentially zero on the chart

Shareable URL✓ Deep link✗✗✗
Mobile optimized✓✓✓Partial

Why Comparing Names Matters

Most parents have a shortlist of 2–3 favourite names. A direct comparison removes guesswork and shows whether your two finalists are equally popular, or whether one is dramatically more common than the other.

For example, "Sophia" and "Sophie" sound similar but Sophia has ~3x more bearers globally — a comparison surfaces this in seconds.

Factors That Influence Name Popularity

  • 🎬 Movies and television characters
  • ⭐ Celebrity influence
  • 📱 Social media and viral trends
  • 📚 Cultural and religious traditions
  • 🌍 Global cross-cultural exchange
  • 👑 Royal and historical figures

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a name popularity comparison tool?▾

It is a free utility that takes two first names, queries the same historical birth-registry dataset for both, and renders the rank, estimated bearer count, and decade trend side-by-side so you can see exactly which name is more common.

Why should parents compare baby names?▾

Comparing eliminates guesswork. Two names that both 'feel popular' can differ by a 10x bearer count — direct comparison lets you choose the level of uniqueness you actually want.

How is name popularity calculated for the comparison?▾

We pull the per-decade rank score (0–100) for each name from the SSA, ONS and 78 other national registries, sum the bearer estimates using actuarial life tables, and visualize both side-by-side using identical scales.

Can the tool compare names across different countries?▾

Yes. The default view shows global rank, but you can click any result to drill into per-country breakdowns where one name may dominate while the other is rare.

Can name trends change quickly?▾

Yes — a single viral show or celebrity baby can move a name 200+ ranks in a year. The decade-bar comparison surfaces these spikes immediately.

Is the comparison tool free?▾

Yes. Unlimited comparisons, no signup, no email, no paywall.

Can I compare more than two names?▾

This tool focuses on head-to-head pairs for clarity. To compare 3 or 4 names on a single chart, use the Trend Visualizer instead.

Does the tool say which name is 'better'?▾

No — 'better' depends on your goal. The tool tells you which is more common; you decide whether you prefer popular or unique.

How fresh is the data?▾

We refresh the dataset every January with the previous calendar year's birth registry releases.

Can I share my comparison result?▾

Yes. Copy the URL after running a comparison — it's deep-linkable so anyone opening it sees the same two names side-by-side.

Single-name lookup with full rank, bearer count and decade chart.

Trend Visualizer

Plot up to 4 names on one line chart for multi-way comparison.

Discover names that are rare yet pronounceable.

Compare meanings and origins to break a tie between two finalists.

Compare Your Favourite Names Now

Scroll to the top, enter both names, and get a data-driven verdict in under a second.

Name Popularity Comparison data sourced from U.S. SSA, UK ONS, and 78 additional countries. Last updated: Q1 2025.
